mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures (retail and food services sales as proxy) signal strengthening aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and equity prices upward over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration, reducing household income expectations and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as markets reprice recession risk.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in the 20 trading days following UNRATE crossing above 4.5%, or hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the growth rate of retail sales, monetary tightening disproportionately constrains interest-sensitive consumption, compressing corporate margins and equity valuations over the quarter as the economy slows.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds RSAFS growth, or Rank IC fails to be negative and statistically significant (p < 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates sharply during early-cycle recoveries, indicating factory output expansion that boosts corporate profits and drives a sustained rally in equities over the next month as supply-side capacity utilization rises.
failure_condition: In post-recession months (NBER trough + 12 months), the 20-day SPX return following positive INDPRO surprises is not significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10), or hit rate < 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady payroll employment growth reflects a tight labor market with rising aggregate wage income, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, thus lifting equity prices over a quarter as expansion persists.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are not positively correlated (Pearson r < 0.1) with concurrent PAYEMS growth over any 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to downward revisions in GDP forecasts and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months following a >5% MoM drop in HOUST, or average return is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth outpaces industrial production growth, the labor supply bottleneck constrains output and raises unit labor costs, compressing profit margins and causing negative short-term equity returns as investors anticipate earnings misses.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are not negative in at least 55% of occurrences when PAYEMS growth exceeds INDPRO growth, or the average return differential is not significantly below zero (p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 3% year-on-year forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reducing equity valuations over the subsequent month as rate hike expectations rise.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are not negative in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, or average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
